item.title: Cyclotron Road Welcomes 11 New Entrepreneurial Fellows
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) has announced the 2026 cohort of scientists and engineers selected to join Cyclotron Road at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Lab). Cyclotron Road is one of four nodes in the DOE Office of Technology Commercialization’s Lab-Embedded Entrepreneurship Program (LEEP) .

item.title: Fermilab installs first beamline component for new state-of-the-art accelerator
item.description: Fermilab crews have successfully placed the first beamline component in the tunnel that will house the powerful new PIP-II linear accelerator, marking a major milestone for the lab’s future neutrino research program.

item.title: Fermilab marks 70 years of neutrino science and leads next-generation experiment
item.description: As Fermilab commemorates 70 years since the discovery of the neutrino at the Savannah River Site in the United States, the lab also celebrates its multiple generations of pioneering neutrino research. Today, Fermilab is building the next era of neutrino discovery through the development of the world-leading Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment.

item.title: Fermilab’s Neutrino Physics Center hosts inaugural DUNE Data Analysis School
item.description: Fermilab hosted the inaugural DUNE Data Analysis School, organized through the lab’s Neutrino Physics Center. The school brought together early-career researchers for intensive training in the software and analysis tools needed to perform physics analyses for the upcoming Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment. The program marks a major step in preparing the scientific workforce for DUNE and is expected to become a recurring training initiative.

item.title: Action! NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
item.description: The 10-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time has officially started, marking the beginning of a new era in astronomy and astrophysics.

item.title: Fermilab’s Vishvas Pandey elected to co-lead international neutrino research collaboration
item.description: Pandey’s term as co-spokesperson of NuSTEC will focus on advancing higher levels of precision for the next generation of neutrino experiments, including the Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment, an international collaboration hosted by Fermilab.

item.title: Linda Cremonesi elected as co-spokesperson for NOvA neutrino experiment
item.description: Currently, NOvA is Fermilab’s leading long-baseline neutrino experiment. Cremonesi is now co-leading the international collaboration of scientists behind it.

item.title: Christina Wang of Fermilab receives prestigious award for advances in dark matter detection
item.description: Christina Wang, a postdoctoral researcher and Lederman Fellow at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory, has been awarded the American Physical Society’s 2026 Mitsuyoshi Tanaka Award in Experimental Particle Physics for her pioneering work in particle detection. Her innovative techniques, which open new pathways for identifying elusive dark matter candidates, are advancing the search for physics beyond the Standard Model.

item.title: Fermilab storage infrastructure enables AI-driven scientific and research discovery for DOE’s Genesis Mission
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission is building a new era of AI-driven scientific discovery — and it requires far more than powerful supercomputers to succeed. To support this national effort, Fermilab’s Fermi Data Platform is providing secure, large-scale data infrastructure needed to make advanced AI research possible across the American Science Cloud.

item.title: Fermilab and Harmoniqs integrate open-source tools to advance qubit control optimization
item.description: Fermilab developed the Quantum Instrumentation Control Kit, or QICK, as a compact, customizable and cost-effective quantum readout and control option for scientists. Harmoniqs, a quantum computing software company, developed Piccolo.jl, a quantum control and calibration software package. Together, their developers are integrating the two to achieve precise, repeatable qubit control, providing better results in less time.

item.title: Podcast: New treatment to fight deadliest cancer
item.description: U.S. National Science Foundation-supported professor Neil Forbes discusses how the bacteria salmonella can be modified for treatment against some of the deadliest cancers...

item.title: Podcast: New security and communications materials
item.description: Nicholas Kotov discusses complex materials and the development of new fused-silk materials with applications in security and communications...

item.title: NSF awards 12 new Regional Innovation Engines to fuel research, jobs and economic growth nationwide
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced it awarded the newest NSF Regional Innovation Engines (NSF Engines) awards to 12 U.S. teams across 20 states. These NSF Engines will build and scale…

item.title: NSF launches Project Triad to advance quantum technology for real-world applications
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation today announced Project Triad, a first-of-its-kind initiative to integrate quantum sensing, quantum networking and quantum computing into a single operational...

item.title: Podcast: The fight against high blood pressure [hypertension]
item.description: U.S. National Science Foundation-supported professor Tao Zhou discusses the development of a new 3D-printed hydrogel-based bioelectronic device for hypertension treatment...

item.title: NSF Discovering Our Universe' gallery to open July 1
item.description: The new "U.S. National Science Foundation Discovering Our Universe" gallery is opening to the public July 1 at the Smithsonian's National Air and Space Museum in Washington, D.C., in celebration of…

item.title: Action! NSF-DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
item.description: The wait is over: NSF–DOE Rubin Observatory, funded by the U.S. National Science Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Office of Science , is now capturing the cosmos...

item.title: Podcast: The future of quantum cryptography
item.description: Secure information is essential in the digital exchanges of modern life. NSF-supported professor Vinod Vaikuntanathan discusses quantum cryptography and the future of encryption...

item.title: Podcast: Brain-computer interface controls exoskeleton
item.description: Payam Heydari discusses a breakthrough brain-computer interface that controls a robotic exoskeleton, technology that could transform the lives of people living with spinal cord injuries...

item.title: NSF selects five additional teams in National Quantum Virtual Laboratory design competition
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation has selected five new teams to design experimental quantum technologies, from networks that can ferry fragile quantum information across long distances to sensors…

item.title: MPS Chemistry Quarterly Office Hours
item.description: NSF MPS holds quarterly office hours to share information with chemistry researchers, administrators and others in the U.S. chemistry research community.

item.title: NSF BIO Virtual Office Hour: Updates from the NSF Directorate for Biological Sciences
item.description: Please join the U.S. National Science Foundation Directorate for Biological Sciences for a directorate-wide virtual office hour.

item.title: NSF BIO Virtual Office Hour: Updates from the NSF Directorate for Biological Sciences
item.description: Please join the U.S. National Science Foundation Directorate for Biological Sciences for a directorate-wide virtual office hour.

item.title: AI for Science: Breaking the boundaries of what science can achieve
item.description: Matoko Taiji talks about

item.title: A magnetic field that kills superconductivity can also bring it back
item.description: A magnetic field that kills superconductivity can also bring it back

item.title: Real-time compression of scientific data with over 8,000-time reduction opens a new paradigm
item.description: Real-time compression of scientific data with over 8,000-time reduction opens a new paradigm

item.title: Discovery of a p-wave magnet in a metal
item.description: RIKEN researchers have found a metallic p-wave magnet that has a commensurate spin helix.

item.title: Theoretical predictions of unusual nonlinear thermoelectric effect confirmed
item.description: RIKEN researchers have observed the nonlinear chiral thermoelectric Hall effect in the semiconductor tellurium.

item.title: Uncovering the link between epigenetic modifications and chromatin structure
item.description: RIKEN researchers have found that histone acetylation directly controls chromatin architecture.

item.title: Long-overlooked gene for deglycosylating enzyme in fish embryo has been revealed
item.description: RIKEN researchers have identified the gene that encodes acid peptide: N-glycanase (PNGase) in zebrafish.

item.title: Quantum geometry lens shines new light on solids
item.description: RIKEN researchers have established bounds for various physical observables in solids by considering the quantum geometric tensor.

item.title: How energy is transferred in photosynthetic bacteria
item.description: RIKEN researchers have analyzed the structure and energy transfer of the phycobilisome–photosystem II megacomplex in a thermophilic cyanobacterium.

item.title: BRCA gene mutations now implicated in thyroid, bladder, skin, and head and neck cancer
item.description: BRCA gene mutations now implicated in thyroid, bladder, skin, and head and neck cancer

item.title: STEM Community Day 2026 | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
item.description: Learn about our science, people, facilities and partners. Discover our history and vision for the future.

item.title: Fusion energy | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
item.description: Harnessing nuclear fusion would bring the clean, unlimited energy of the stars to Earth.

item.title: LCLS-II High Energy Upgrade | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
item.description: Now, we’re turning up the energy. The high-energy upgrade to LCLS will extend its ultrafast megahertz capability into the “hard,” or high-energy, X-ray regime.

item.title: Action! NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
item.description: The 10-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time has officially started, marking the beginning of a new era in astronomy and astrophysics.

item.title: SLAC researchers ‘watch’ molecules steered by laser light
item.description: Using the powerful LCLS X-ray laser, researchers at SLAC have directly imaged for the first time how molecules rearrange during a chemical reaction controlled by light.

item.title: SLAC’s premier facilities and record-setting datasets fuel DOE Genesis Mission
item.description: The lab’s contributions to the national AI initiative are bolstered by its breakthrough scientific tools, unprecedented data and unique partnerships that help illuminate nature from the expansion of the universe to the motions of electrons.

item.title: Building the quantum toolbox
item.description: As QuantISED enters its next phase in its effort to develop quantum technologies capable of exploring some of the universe’s biggest unanswered questions, scientists reflect on what the first round made possible and what comes next.

item.title: SLAC and Stanford researchers find an inexpensive way to make batteries last longer
item.description: By adjusting the heating process when making lithium-ion cathodes, the team created batteries that retained nearly 93% of their energy after 500 cycles.

item.title: Researchers pioneer method to rapidly design proteins
item.description: Their strategy revealed new details that open the door to designing proteins with powerful abilities that could ultimately benefit medicine and manufacturing.

item.title: Groundbreaking instrument back online, marking a key milestone in high-energy upgrade to SLAC’s X-ray laser
item.description: The X-ray Pump Probe instrument is returning to normal operations this spring, anticipating a major capability boost when the high-energy beam comes online.

item.title: News – Home | CERN
item.description: No posts were found. Try to change the category or the date filters.
